key_insight: Surface-level diversity in LLM math reasoning does not reliably reflect true strategy diversity, so models can look diverse while still solving problems in similar ways.
engineering_relevance: For test-time scaling and RLVR, the right objective is approach-level diversity: it can improve performance when sampling candidates, but naive diversity optimization may fail to generalize to real reasoning variety.
method: Introduced approach-level diversity as variation across correct solution strategies for the same math problem.
Built a human-calibrated LLM judge to compare surface-level and approach-level diversity.
Analyzed diversity-aware RLVR and test-time candidate sampling under the new metric.
evidence_signal: Across evaluations, prior diversity metrics were unreliable proxies for approach-level diversity, and optimizing judge-based diversity rewards preserved target scores while reducing true strategy diversity.
watch_item: Directly optimizing an LLM judge for diversity can be gamed by model-specific quirks, so judge design and calibration are critical.
paper_title: Are We Measuring Strategy or Phrasing? The Gap Between Surface- and Approach-Level Diversity in LLM Math Reasoning
